footer.description: Bringing native Node.js environments directly to your browser tab. Fast, secure, and entirely local.

hero.desc: Run Node.js directly in the browser. Instant environments, zero setup. Boot a full-stack app inside the browser with native performance.

features.f1_desc: Environments start in milliseconds. No containers to provision, no images to pull.
features.f2_desc: Run Express, Vite, Next.js, and more. A fully compliant Node.js runtime inside your browser tab.
features.f3_desc: Work offline. Compute stays local. Secure by design with zero backend dependencies required.

details.title:
Embeddable IDEs.
Interactive Docs.
details.desc: Integrate Runboxjs into your platform to provide interactive code examples, tutorials, or full-fledged development environments. It just works.

cta.title: Start building.
cta.desc: Add Runboxjs to your project and experience the power of in-browser Node.js environments.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Everything you need to know about integrating Runboxjs into your platform.

cards.c1_desc: Get up and running in less than 5 minutes.
cards.c2_desc: Detailed documentation of the Runboxjs API.
cards.c3_desc: Step-by-step tutorials for common use cases.

install.title: Installation
install.desc: Install the package using your preferred package manager:

quickstart.title: Quick Start
quickstart.desc: Create a RunboxInstance, write files, and execute commands. The WebAssembly module initializes automatically on import:

advanced.title: Advanced: Interactive Terminal
advanced.desc: Runboxjs provides full terminal emulation capabilities. You can send inputs exactly as if a user was typing in an xterm.js instance:

overview.title: Overview
overview.desc: RunboxJS is a WebAssembly-first runtime that lets you execute project workflows directly in-browser: files, commands, terminal streams, package installs, git flows, and assistant tool calls.
overview.d2: Virtual filesystem + runtime dispatch by command target
overview.d4: Interactive docs, playgrounds, browser IDEs, AI coding copilots

preview.no_server_desc: Click the Run button to execute your code. If it starts an HTTP server, the preview will automatically appear here.

output.server_ready: Server ready - navigate with the browser bar above
output.workspace_reset: Workspace reset to the default template.

confirm.reset_message: All local changes will be lost.

editor.empty: Select or create a file to edit

react_dashboard.name: React Dashboard
react_dashboard.description: React app with routes (SSR), reusable components, and npm dependencies.

vanilla_js.name: Vanilla JS App
vanilla_js.description: Simple HTTP app executed by bun with local modules.

typescript_api.name: TypeScript API
typescript_api.description: Node-compatible TypeScript server executed with Bun shim in Runbox.

python_data_lab.name: Python Data Lab
python_data_lab.description: Demonstrates execution with python and pip install/list/freeze.

git_workflow.name: Git Workflow
git_workflow.description: Runs init/branch/commit/merge commands before starting the app.

pnpm_showcase.name: pnpm Showcase
pnpm_showcase.description: Uses pnpm install/list/audit and exposes data with dependencies.

yarn_showcase.name: Yarn Showcase
yarn_showcase.description: Runs yarn install/outdated and starts a small SSR page.
